---
title: "Modern Creel Survey Analysis in R"
subtitle: "Design, estimation, diagnostics, and reporting with tidycreel"
toc: false
---

::: {.book-hero}
## A methods-first book for creel survey analysis

This book guides readers through modern creel survey analysis in R. It is designed as a companion to `tidycreel`, but it is broader than a package manual.

The emphasis is on design-based reasoning, clear estimands, data structure, uncertainty, and interpretation that stays within the covered domain.

::: {.book-pill-row}
<span class="book-pill">Quarto book</span>
<span class="book-pill">Design-based inference</span>
<span class="book-pill">Editorial but practical</span>
<span class="book-pill">Companion to tidycreel</span>
:::

<a class="book-cta" href="preface.html">Start with the Preface</a>
:::

## What to expect

::: {.book-grid}
::: {.book-card}
### Front matter
Orientation pages that explain the book's scope and reading path.
:::

::: {.book-card}
### Part-based structure
Foundations, survey designs, data structures, estimation, and case studies.
:::

::: {.book-card}
### Consistent chapter pattern
Each chapter states its purpose, key ideas, support status, and example focus.
:::

::: {.book-card}
### Online-first layout
The site is structured as a Quarto book, with the cover, parts, and chapter navigation visible in the rendered site.
:::
:::

## Start here

Begin with the Preface, then read the Foundations section in order.

If you already know the background, you can move directly to the design chapters that match your survey type.

::: {.book-note}
The online layout is intentionally Quarto-native. It is designed to feel like a published book site with clear front matter, grouped parts, and a clean reading path.
:::

## Book layout

This site is organized into these parts:

- Foundations
- Survey Designs
- Data Structures and Validation
- Estimation and Reporting
- Case Studies
